changes.change_condition: For use with TLS 1.3
changes.change_action: must be selected from the cipher suites defined for TLS 1.3; TLS 1.2 and lower cipher suites must not be used
changes.evidence:
This specification defines the following cipher suites for use with TLS 1.3.

... | TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256       | {0x13,0x01} |
... | TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384       | {0x13,0x02} |
... | TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256 | {0x13,0x03} |
... | TLS_AES_128_CCM_SHA256       | {0x13,0x04} |
... | TLS_AES_128_CCM_8_SHA256     | {0x13,0x05} |

Although TLS 1.3 uses the same cipher suite space as previous versions of TLS, TLS 1.3 cipher suites are defined differently, only specifying the symmetric ciphers, and cannot be used for TLS 1.2. Similarly, cipher suites for TLS 1.2 and lower cannot be used with TLS 1.3.

changes.change_condition: endpoint receives an extension response for which it did not send the corresponding extension request
changes.evidence: Upon receiving such an extension, an endpoint MUST abort the handshake with an "unsupported_extension" alert.

changes.change_condition: implementation receives a recognized extension in a message where that extension is not specified to appear
changes.evidence: If an implementation receives an extension which it recognizes and which is not specified for the message in which it appears, it MUST abort the handshake with an "illegal_parameter" alert.

changes.change_condition: sending extension responses when the remote endpoint did not send the corresponding extension requests
changes.evidence: Implementations MUST NOT send extension responses if the remote endpoint did not send the corresponding extension requests, with the exception of the "cookie" extension in the HelloRetryRequest.

changes.new_value: values with the first byte in the range 0-254 (decimal) are assigned via Specification Required
changes.evidence: Values with the first byte in the range 0-254 (decimal) are assigned via Specification Required [RFC8126].

changes.change_action: must not use assigned allocation; reserved for private use
changes.new_value: values with the first byte 255 (decimal) are reserved for Private Use
changes.evidence: Values with the first byte 255 (decimal) are reserved for Private Use [RFC8126].

changes.change_condition: within a given extension block

changes.evidence: Clients MUST NOT offer multiple KeyShareEntry values for the same group.

changes.change_action: set to byte string public value with fixed length
changes.new_value: 32 bytes for X25519 and 56 bytes for X448
changes.evidence: For X25519 and X448, the contents of the public value are the byte string inputs and outputs of the corresponding functions defined in [RFC7748]: 32 bytes for X25519 and 56 bytes for X448.

changes.change_condition: For X25519 and X448 after computing the Diffie-Hellman shared secret

changes.change_condition: For X25519 and X448 when computing the ECDH shared secret
changes.new_value: the result of applying the ECDH scalar multiplication function to the secret key and the peer's public key; the output is used raw, with no processing
changes.evidence: The ECDH shared secret is the result of applying the ECDH scalar multiplication function to the secret key (into scalar input) and the peer's public key (into u-coordinate point input).  The output is used raw, with no processing.

changes.change_condition: For X25519 and X448 when forming the public key to put into KeyShareEntry.key_exchange
changes.new_value: the result of applying the ECDH scalar multiplication function to the secret key of appropriate length and the standard public basepoint
changes.evidence: The public key to put into the KeyShareEntry.key_exchange structure is the result of applying the ECDH scalar multiplication function to the secret key of appropriate length (into scalar input) and the standard public basepoint (into u-coordinate point input).

changes.change_condition: For finite field groups during Diffie-Hellman shared secret calculation
changes.new_value: The negotiated key (Z) is converted to a byte string by encoding in big-endian form and left-padded with zeros up to the size of the prime; this byte string is used as the shared secret
changes.evidence: The negotiated key (Z) is converted to a byte string by encoding in big-endian form and left-padded with zeros up to the size of the prime.  This byte string is used as the shared secret in the key schedule as specified above.

changes.related_state_or_step: server handling of client certificate response
changes.evidence: If the client ignored some of the required certificate extension OIDs and supplied a certificate that does not satisfy the request, the server MAY at its discretion either continue the connection without client authentication or abort the handshake with an "unsupported_certificate" alert.

changes.change_condition: if the client cannot construct an acceptable chain using the provided certificates and decides to abort the handshake
changes.evidence: If the client cannot construct an acceptable chain using the provided certificates and decides to abort the handshake, then it MUST abort the handshake with an appropriate certificate-related alert (by default, "unsupported_certificate"; see Section 6.2 for more information).

changes.evidence: The verify_data value is computed as follows: verify_data = HMAC(finished_key, Transcript-Hash(Handshake Context, Certificate*, CertificateVerify*))

changes.new_value: the shared secret is the x-coordinate of the ECDH shared secret elliptic curve point represented as an octet string
changes.evidence: the shared secret is the x-coordinate of the ECDH shared secret elliptic curve point represented as an octet string.

changes.change_condition: For secp256r1, secp384r1, and secp521r1 in ECDHE
changes.change_action: set to binary representation in network byte order with fixed curve-implied length and left-zero padding if necessary
changes.new_value: binary representation of the x value in network byte order
changes.evidence: X and Y, respectively, are the binary representations of the x and y values in network byte order. There are no internal length markers, so each number representation occupies as many octets as implied by the curve parameters. For P-256, this means that each of X and Y use 32 octets, padded on the left by zeros if necessary. For P-384, they take 48 octets each. For P-521, they take 66 octets each.

For P-256, this means that each of X and Y use 32 octets, padded on the left by zeros if necessary. For P-384, they take 48 octets each. For P-521, they take 66 octets each.

changes.change_condition: When validating Diffie-Hellman public key from peer
changes.evidence: Peers MUST validate each other's public key Y by ensuring that 1 < Y < p-1.

changes.change_condition: When validating ECDHE public value Q for secp256r1, secp384r1, and secp521r1

changes.change_condition: For secp256r1, secp384r1, and secp521r1 when representing the ECDH shared secret octet string
changes.evidence: leading zeros found in this octet string MUST NOT be truncated.

changes.change_condition: When converting the negotiated key (Z) for finite field groups
changes.new_value: left-padded with zeros up to the size of the prime
changes.evidence: The negotiated key (Z) is converted to a byte string by encoding in big-endian form and left-padded with zeros up to the size of the prime.

changes.change_condition: padding is present in encrypted TLS record
changes.evidence: The design also enforces all-zero padding octets, which allows for quick detection of padding errors.

changes.change_condition: when forming the per-record nonce
changes.evidence: The 64-bit record sequence number is encoded in network byte order and padded to the left with zeros to iv_length.

changes.change_condition: when generating TLSCiphertext padding
changes.evidence: Implementations MUST set the padding octets to all zeros before encrypting.

changes.change_condition: when padding TLSInnerPlaintext
changes.evidence: zeros:  An arbitrary-length run of zero-valued bytes may appear in the cleartext after the type field.
